items.title: Mega project, limited public profile
items.summary: Helios also claimed to have a floating solar project in Sembawang, Singapore and is in talks with Cambodia's authorities for solar industrial parks there. The solar park will feature a 1,000-megawatt-peak solar photovoltaic plant paired with a 1,600-megawatt-hour battery energy storage system. Helios chairman Datuk Seri Ibrahim Muhammad said the project is being developed under the government's Corporate Renewable Energy Supply Scheme.

items.title: MACC seeking UK evidence in probe into international law firm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is working to obtain evidence from the United Kingdom as part of its probe into international law firm White & Case over its alleged links to the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal. MACC Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki told the New Straits Times that investigators had identified elements of offences under Malaysian law, including provisions under the MACC Act 2009 and the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001. The firm has been linked to the alleged misappropriation of US$1 billion in 1MDB funds, including the diversion of about US$700 million to an offshore entity beyond 1MDB's control.

items.title: Give up PPR units for those more deserving, says former tenant
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: After 13 years in a People's Housing Programme (PPR) unit, Nazrimah Jamal Abidin has turned a new chapter in her life — moving out of the low-cost unit and into her own home. Nazrimah, 51, who lived at the Taman Manggis PPR, said her journey reflected perseverance and the importance of fair access to public housing. My husband earned a daily wage and we were struggling," she said Nazrimah said she left the PPR in 2019 after securing a rent-to-own home under a state scheme, to be paid over 20 years.

items.title: Trump suspends Iran bombing for two weeks, after apocalyptic threats
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday he was suspending bombing of Iran for two weeks but that Teheran must reopen the key Strait of Hormuz, barely an hour before his apocalyptic deadline to destroy the country was set to expire. After more than five weeks of blistering attacks on Iran by the United States and Israel, Trump said he had accepted a proposal mediated by Pakistan to extend his deadline but he again pushed on the Strait of Hormuz, the waterway vital for the world's oil. Trump said he had spoke to Pakistan's leaders who "requested that I hold off the destructive force being sent tonight to Iran."

items.title: Rising living costs leave Penang's public housing threshold outdated, warns expert
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The RM1,500 household income threshold for People's Housing Programme (PPR) in the state has been questioned by experts for failing to reflect rising costs of living. "But with inflation and rising housing costs, households earning just above RM1,500 can still face financial hardship," he said. "Transitional support, including tiered subsidies, financial advice and job aid, can help families achieve financial stability."

items.title: 'Screams of delight': Artemis crew flying home to thrilled NASA scientists
items.summary: HOUSTON, (Texas): The Artemis II astronauts were jetting towards Earth and sharing their lunar flyby souvenirs Tuesday, delighting colleagues both at home and in space with captivating tales of their historic journey. The astronauts were busy Tuesday afternoon debriefing lunar scientists in Houston's mission control about what they had witnessed during their nearly seven-hour observation period of the Moon. said Hansen.

items.title: HBA backs firm action on PPR subletting abuse
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The National House Buyers Association (HBA) has called for stricter enforcement against abuse of People's Housing Programme (PPR) units, while urging a measured approach towards tenants who may no longer qualify. Honorary secretary-general Datuk Chang Kim Loong said firm action was needed to ensure subsidised housing reached intended low-income groups. "HBA supports the authorities' firm stand, which benefits future tenants, especially given the long queue for social housing meant for eligible groups," he said.

items.title: Cheras karaoke riot signals rising criminal boldness, says criminologist
items.summary: The incident saw attackers forcing their way into a karaoke centre using a lorry before causing extensive damage and fleeing in several cars, Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said yesterday police acted swiftly to arrest 12 people. However, an expert said the fact that the incident took place showed a troubling shift towards more brazen and organised forms of crime. Senior criminologist Datuk Dr P. Sundramoorthy said the incident pointed to a level of coordination and intent that went beyond spontaneous violence.

items.title: Klang security guard dies after assailant returns a second time to attack
items.summary: He, however, returned alone minutes later and attacked Anand with a steering lock," said Viswa when met at Anand's funeral at Fairy Park Heritage City yesterday. He said the blow to Anand's rib area proved fatal. '," he said.

items.title: Israel army 'regrets' damage to Teheran synagogue, says targeted senior commander
items.summary: JERUSALEM: The Israeli military on Tuesday expressed regret over "collateral damage" to a synagogue in Tehran caused by an overnight strike that it said was targeting a senior Iranian commander. "Last night (Tuesday), the IDF struck a senior commander in the emergency headquarters 'Khatam al-Anbiya' of the Iranian regime," an Israeli military spokesperson said in response to a question from AFP. "The IDF regrets the collateral damage to the synagogue and emphasises that the strike was targeted at a senior military target within the regime's armed forces, not at any place of worship."

items.title: Petronas tanker picking up speed after clearing Hormuz Strait
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Petronas tanker carrying Iraqi crude oil has picked up speed in the Arabian Sea, about 200 nautical miles off Oman, and is expected to arrive in Pengerang on April 17, Marine Traffic showed. The Ocean Thunder , chartered by Petco, a unit of Petronas, is among seven Malaysia-linked vessels cleared by Iran to transit the Strait of Hormuz. The tanker left Al Basrah Port in southern Iraq on April 3.

items.title: Tahfiz schools lift Pahang to 2nd place in 2025 SPM
items.summary: KUANTAN: Outstanding performances in the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) by Maahad Tahfiz Negeri Pahang (MTNP) and Maahad Tahfiz Turath Negeri Pahang (MTTNP), both under Yayasan Pahang, have helped the state achieve its best-ever state average grade, placing it second nationwide. MTTNP emerged as the top school in both Pahang and the country, while MTNP was ranked second in the state and placed among the top five nationwide. Unlike typical science stream students who usually take about nine subjects, students at MTNP often handle 11 to 12 subjects.

items.title: How cutting one litre of RON95 daily can save millions: Simple habits to ease Malaysia’s fuel subsidy burden
items.summary: According to available data, Malaysians consume roughly between five and six litres of fuel daily on average. With an estimated 10 million vehicles on the road each day, national fuel consumption is believed to be in the region of 50 to 60 million litres daily. Walking or cycling instead of driving a 2km trip to nearby shops could save about 0.2 to 0.4 litres of fuel.

items.title: Madani Mart vs KR1M: Same same but different?
items.summary: So how will Madani Mart be different? In contrast, Madani Mart operates on a private licensing model, according to Yayasan Madani, the programme’s backer. Yayasan Madani said the Madani Mart will use a “decentralised ecosystem”.

items.title: From sea jacuzzi to pickleball on water: Penang unveils new tourism site Forest Island
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 8 — A new tourism product in Penang will offer visitors experiences in the middle of the sea, ranging from a sea jacuzzi and water sports to scenic views of the island’s coastline. Forest Island is a purpose-built offshore attraction offering sea-based leisure experiences. Visitors enjoy a sea jacuzzi experience during a boat ride to Forest Island.

items.title: Semporna paradise under siege: Locals battle pollution and foreign domination of tourism
items.summary: SEMPORNA, April 8 — On a glossy postcard, Semporna, located on the southeastern coast of Sabah off the Celebes Sea, is sold as the ultimate bucket-list destination. Hordes of tourists, particularly scuba divers and snorkelers, travel thousands of miles to experience the crystal-clear waters and vibrant coral reefs of the islands off Semporna town. Trash For those working on the frontlines of Semporna’s tourism industry, the sea pollution is impossible to ignore.

items.title: French couple leave Iran after more than three years in detention
items.summary: PARIS: Two French nationals were headed home from war-stricken Iran on Tuesday after spending more than three years in an Iranian prison on espionage charges, French President Emmanuel Macron said. "Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris are free and on their way to France, after three and a half years in detention in Iran," the French president wrote on X. They were the last two French nationals being held in Iran.

items.title: To Lam elected president
items.summary: VIETNAM'S lawmakers yesterday unanimously elected Communist Party secretary-general To Lam as the country's state president for the next five years, making him the most powerful Vietnamese leader in decades. The widely anticipated move marks a break from Vietnam's traditional collective leadership system, consolidating authority in one figure in ways analysts say could tilt the one-party state towards greater authoritarianism, while also enabling faster decision-making, similar to its neighbour China. "Concentrating greater power in To Lam's hands could pose risks to Vietnam's political system, such as increased authoritarianism," said Le Hong Hiep, senior fellow at the ISEAS Yusof Ishak Institute in Singapore.

items.title: AI disruption dims outlook for smartphone market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The global smartphone market is set to face a sharper downturn as artificial intelligence (AI) reshapes demand and pressures manufacturers, according to MBSB Investment Bank Bhd (MBSB Research). The weakening smartphone demand alongside broader supply chain and geopolitical risks prompted MBSB Research to maintain its "neutral" view on the technology sector. "Global smartphone demand continues to soften as AI-driven device upgrades and pricing pressures reshape the market dynamics," MBSB Research said.

items.title: Perhilitan tracking tiger after paw prints found at farm
items.summary: KLUANG: The Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) has stepped up patrols following the discovery of tiger paw prints at a farm here last Friday. Perhilitan director-general Datuk Abdul Kadir Abu Hashim said a four-member team from the Batu Pahat and Kluang Perhilitan offices confirmed that the prints belonged to a Malayan tiger. Its Malayan Tiger Conservation Unit will also deploy camera traps to track the animal's movements and determine the appropriate follow-up action.

items.title: Govt revives plan for independent National Harmony Commission
items.summary: THE government is reviving a plan to establish an independent National Harmony Commission, with the National Unity Ministry backing a renewed proposal amid growing concerns over unity-related issues. The ministry said the move followed a proposal by activist Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye and builds on earlier efforts dating back to 2015, when the National Unity Advisory Council drafted three bills on racial and religious hatred offences, national harmony and reconciliation and the establishment of a commission. While the commission proposal was agreed to in principle, it later stalled over jurisdictional concerns involving agencies such as the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia and the police.

items.title: UK govt bars Kanye West, forcing cancellation of festival
items.summary: LONDON: The UK government on Tuesday blocked US rapper Kanye West from entering the country due to his past antisemitic outbursts, prompting organisers of a festival he was to headline to cancel the July event. The organisers of the Wireless Festival in London where West – now known as Ye – had been booked to play all three nights in July swiftly cancelled the event. "As a result of the Home Office banning YE from entering the United Kingdom, Wireless Festival has been forced to cancel," organisers said on Instagram.

items.title: Iran cuts direct diplomacy with US as Trump's deadline looms
items.summary: ANKARA: Iran has cut off direct communications with the US following President Donald Trump's threat to destroy Iran's "whole civilisation", Anadolu Ajansi reported, citing media reports on Tuesday. The move came ahead of a Tuesday deadline set by Trump for a potential agreement, complicating efforts to reach a deal but not ending ongoing negotiations, according to The New York Times, citing three Iranian officials. Officials said indirect talks through ceasefire mediators are continuing despite the suspension of direct contact, according to the reporting, which was largely confirmed by The Wall Street Journal.

items.title: 'Only the president knows' what he'll do on Iran -White House
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Donald Trump is the only person who knows his plans for Iran, the White House said, after the US president warned that a "whole civilisation will die' if Tehran fails to reach a deal Tuesday. "The Iranian regime has until 8pm Eastern Time to meet the moment and make a deal with the United States," Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said in a statement to AFP, when asked if Trump was prepared to use a nuclear weapon, and about a report that Iran had cut off negotiations. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Rantau Panjang villagers buy water tank as dry taps persist
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: Villagers in Kampung Che Daah, Gual Tok Deh, have pooled their money to buy a 400-litre water tank costing RM430, as months of dry taps continue to disrupt daily life. The community has been relying on the state government's water operator, Air Kelantan Sdn Bhd (AKSB), to fill the tank whenever supply trucks arrive. Villager Muhamed Dollah, 64, said the water shortage began months before Ramadan and remains unresolved.

items.title: Netanyahu says Israel struck railways, bridges in Iran
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israel struck on Tuesday railways and bridges in Iran "used by the Revolutionary Guards", after Iranian officials reported damage to at least two bridges and railway infrastructure. Today they struck the railways and bridges used by the Revolutionary Guards." Israeli military spokesman Brigadier General Effie Defrin said "air force pilots continue to deepen the damage to the Iranian terror regime."

items.title: NST receives top English-language coverage award from UPM
items.summary: SERDANG: The New Straits Times has received the Highest English-Language Coverage Award at the Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM) Media Engagement and Promotion Ceremony 2026 here tonight. Besides NST, other media organisations also received awards at the ceremony. Berita Harian received the Highest Bahasa Melayu-Language Coverage Award and the Print Media Visibility Award, while Harian Metro received the Highest Bahasa Melayu-Language Coverage Award and the Highest Research News Coverage Award.

items.title: Power cut off on Iran's Kharg Island after heavy US strikes: Reports
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Power was cut off on Iran's Kharg Island in the Persian Gulf on Tuesday following a wave of US strikes targeting more than 50 sites, Anadolu Ajansi reported, citing US and Iranian media. Iranian outlets said the power outage came after heavy US attacks on the island. The Wall Street Journal said that US fighter jets bombed more than 50 military targets on the Iranian island.

items.title: UPM strengthens food security through technology, youth involvement
items.summary: SERDANG: Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM) is helping to strengthen the national food security agenda through the use of modern technology and the involvement of the younger generation to enhance agricultural productivity and the quality of produce. UPM vice-chancellor Datuk Prof Dr Ahmad Farhan Mohd Sadullah said the country's reliance on imported food and global geopolitical uncertainties underscored the importance of having domestic capability in food production to ensure a stable supply. He said the use of technology in the agricultural sector was among the university's key strategies to increase output, accelerate production processes and reduce operating costs.

items.title: Timeline: Key events since Trump's first Hormuz deadline to Iran
items.summary: On March 21, Trump gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the strait, warning that US forces would strike the country's energy facilities if it failed to comply. On March 27, he extended the deadline further to April 6. On April 2, US and Israeli forces targeted the B1 Bridge linking Tehran and Karaj.

items.title: MTEN meeting reviews impact of global energy crisis, focus on protecting the people - Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting held today reviewed the impact of the global energy crisis, with the main focus on protecting the people and ensuring the security of the country's strategic supplies. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said efforts to curb leakages and smuggling would continue to be strengthened through tighter supply chain monitoring and the integrated implementation of Op Tiris 4.0 involving various enforcement agencies. Anwar, who is also the finance minister, said that in the long term, the government would strengthen strategic buffer stocks, develop local industries and formulate a comprehensive policy through MyMedSecure.

items.title: China's electronics manufacturer learns to live with Trump tariffs and thrive
items.summary: Agilian Technology, which makes products mostly for Western brands, saw its US orders — accounting for more than half its revenue — frozen for months and clients demanded it set up production outside China. China's trade surplus for the first two months of 2026 rose to US$213.6 billion, official data showed, from US$169.21 billion a year earlier. China retaliated.

items.title: Hormuz closure a boon for some Gulf states
items.summary: THE Strait of Hormuz's closure and the resulting surge in global oil prices have handed financial windfalls to Iran, Oman and Saudi Arabia, while other states that lack alternative shipment routes have lost billions of dollars, a Reuters analysis found. Reuters' analysis of March export data found Iraq and Kuwait's estimated notional oil export revenues both plunged by about three-quarters year-on-year. Saudi Arabia's oil revenues increased by 4.3 per cent, while the UAE's dipped by 2.6 per cent as the price surge offset lower volumes.

items.title: NST Leader: Of PPR and complexity of urban poverty
items.summary: THE Penang state government is seeking to evict ineligible occupants of the People's Public Housing Programme (PPR) to free up the units for the more deserving. A strict eligibility cutoff — such as the RM1,500 household income threshold — may fail to capture the complexity of urban poverty, where even slightly higher incomes can still leave families in a financially precarious state. Long-term occupants could be offered structured exit plans, including priority access to affordable housing schemes or financial assistance.

items.title: Malaysia urged to diversify energy sources amid Middle East crisis and rising oil prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 8 — Rising petroleum import prices and disruptions to global logistics caused by the Middle East conflict, which threaten inflation rates and the country’s long-term fiscal stability, serve as a warning for Malaysia to strengthen energy sovereignty by diversifying local energy sources. Universiti Malaysia Terengganu (UMT) vice-chancellor Prof Dr Mohd Zamri Ibrahim said diversifying local energy sources would also reduce the country’s dependence on volatile fossil fuel markets. “Green hydrogen is no longer a ‘technology of the future’ but a present national security necessity.

items.title: In disrupted speech, Azam Baki says strong leadership needed to fight corruption
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Effective success in combating corruption does not rely solely on the strength of laws, but requires firm leadership and the cultivation of a comprehensive culture of integrity, says Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki. He said even the most robust system would fail if leadership were weak and compromised with corrupt practices. According to Azam, MACC was implementing a three-pronged approach which encompassed strengthening effective enforcement, comprehensive prevention and efficient management.

items.title: ‘I was not aware’: Dzulkefly denies role in daughter’s board posts amid RM5m suit against Najib
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad told the High Court here today that he was unaware of his eldest daughter Nurul Iman’s appointments to two companies, which allegedly occurred before and after he became minister. Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Tun Razak arrives at the High Court to continue proceedings in his defamation suit involving Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad over a Facebook post on cronyism, Kuala Lumpur, April 7, 2026. — Bernama pic In his witness statement, Dzulkefly said he had never influenced Nurul Iman’s appointment at AIM Solutions.

items.title: Govt to establish stockpile of medicines, medical devices amid Middle East conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The government will establish a national strategic buffer stock to ensure the supply of medicines and medical devices following the global energy crisis caused by the conflict in West Asia. “The global energy crisis not only affects oil and logistics, but also the supply of medicines and medical devices. In addition, Akmal Nasrullah said the government will implement the Special Access Pathway as a short-term measure to ensure the supply of critical medicines and medical devices in the country remains secure.

items.title: Police: UPSI bowling centre cleaner died of sudden heart attack, no foul play
items.summary: Muallim police chief Supt Khalid Mohamad said the district’s police headquarters received information about the incident via the NG999 system at 1.57am today. He said a police team was dispatched to the scene and found that the Ministry of Health (MOH) and the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) personnel were already there, with the 53-year-old victim found unconscious in a utility room. Preliminary investigation at the scene found no trace of criminal elements or suspicious injuries on the victim’s body.

items.title: Arrest warrants out against Daim Zainuddin’s two sons and their associate for skipping MACC investigation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The Magistrate’s Court today issued arrest warrants against three individuals, including two sons of the late Tun Daim Zainuddin, for failing to comply with notices from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to face charges today. The prosecution’s application has merit, and therefore, the arrest warrants are issued,” she said. Therefore, MACC filed this application for arrest warrants in court,” said Illi Marisqa.

items.title: B100 pilot shows promise at RM4.50 per litre, Felda plans wider rollout to boost energy security
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — B100, a 100 per cent palm oil-based biodiesel, has the potential to emerge as a more competitive and sustainable alternative energy source amid uncertainties arising from the West Asia crisis, said Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek. Ahmad Shabery said he has brought up the matter with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. B100’s factory price is estimated at about RM4.50 per litre, depending on CPO prices, Ahmad Shabery said.

items.title: From housewife to lawyer: Woman without SPM finds purpose after son’s murder charge ordeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A woman’s life changed dramatically after her son was charged in a murder case, prompting her to pursue a law career despite not having completed Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM). Azlina Abdul Aziz, 52, said the 2016 ordeal marked a turning point in her life. Azlina said she was a full-time housewife until her son’s arrest in 2016.

items.title: Kajang man jailed six months after failing to pay fine for abusing kitten at laundry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A 49-year-old man who went viral for throwing a kitten against a wall at a self-service laundry was sentenced to six months in jail today after failing to pay a RM60,000 fine imposed by the Sessions Court here. Judge Khainur Aleeza Ismail had earlier sentenced Toh Teck Hooi to the fine and stipulated that failure to pay would result in six months’ imprisonment, according to Harian Metro . The next day, Selangor JPV Animal Welfare officers inspected the site.

items.title: All universities corrupt? One or two cases is too broad stroke, says UTM chief
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 7 — Claims that higher education institutions (IPT) are breeding grounds for corruption have been called irresponsible, as they risk undermining and tarnishing the reputation of the country’s education system. Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) vice-chancellor Prof Dr Mohd Shafry Mohd Rahim said that although there have been isolated cases, they cannot be used as a basis to label all IPTs. Mohd Shafry said every government agency or organisation, including universities, has clear integrity and governance systems, including in procurement processes.

items.title: Protesters briefly disrupt Azam Baki’s keynote address at WTC in Kuala Lumpur (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — A keynote address by Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki was momentarily interrupted when two men entered a hall with banners during the Wacana Ilmiah Perdana programme at the World Trade Centre (WTC) here. According to a report in The Star , witnesses said that one of the men shouted and rushed towards the front of the stage, causing a brief commotion among attendees. Despite the disruption, Azam continued his speech professionally, even making a lighthearted remark to ease tension.

items.title: No special circumstances: Federal Court denies Zuraida’s bid to revisit PKR bond reduction
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 7 — The Federal Court here today dismissed former PKR vice-president Datuk Zuraida Kamaruddin’s application to review its earlier decision granting PKR leave to appeal against the appellate court’s ruling to reduce the bond payment from RM10 million to RM100,000 in a party bond breach case. On June 24, last year, the Federal Court granted PKR leave to appeal against the matter. On Dec 11, 2024, the appellate court three-man bench led by Judge Datuk See Mee Chun upheld the High Court’s ruling that Zuraida had breached the bond but reduced the quantum payment from RM10 million to RM100,000.

items.title: Iran says ‘one by one’ for passage of Malaysia’s six remaining ships stuck at Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The remaining six Malaysian vessels stranded in the Strait of Hormuz will be cleared to pass through the waterway in stages, Iran’s Ambassador to Malaysia Valiollah Mohammadi Nasrabadi said. Earlier, the Malaysian Foreign Ministry confirmed that one of the seven Malaysian-owned vessels previously stranded in the Strait of Hormuz due to the evolving regional situation had been granted passage and was proceeding to its final destination. Iran has allowed passage through the Strait of Hormuz to vessels from “non-hostile” nations, affirming that it will facilitate to ensure their safe passage.

items.title: Defence mega projects to go ahead despite energy crisis as delays could hike costs, says deputy minister
items.summary: LUMUT, April 7 — Mega projects under the Ministry of Defence will continue as planned, including the Royal Malaysian Navy’s (RMN) Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project, despite the ongoing global energy crisis. Deputy Defence Minister Adly Zahari said the projects will proceed according to the schedule outlined in the 13th Malaysia Plan, as they are critical to national security and sovereignty. Meanwhile, Adly said the ministry will identify cost-saving measures to be implemented without affecting the Malaysian Armed Forces’ (MAF) operations and training.

items.title: Storm alert: Seven states plus Labuan brace for heavy rain, strong winds until 5pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected over seven states, as well as the entire Federal Territory of Labuan, until 5 pm today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said such weather conditions are expected in Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar and Batang Padang), Kelantan (Jeli, Tanah Merah, Machang, Pasir Puteh, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang), Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Hulu Terengganu, Dungun and Kemaman), Pahang (Tanah Tinggi Cameron, Lipis, Jerantut, Kuantan, Pekan and Rompin) and Johor (Tangkak, Muar, Mersing, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru). Sarawak will also be affected, involving Sarikei (Meradong), Sibu (Sibu dan Selangau), Mukah, Kapit (Song, Kapit dan Bukit Mabong), Bintulu (Tatau dan Sebauh) and Limbang, while Sabah, involving Pedalaman (Sipitang, Kuala Penyu, Beaufort and Tambunan), as well as the west coast areas.

items.title: Malaysian palm oil producers want Putrajaya to match Asean peers with stronger biodiesel use
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — The Malaysian Biodiesel Association (MBA) has called on the government to follow the proactive measures announced by other Asean counterparts and expedite the nationwide implementation of higher blends under the national biodiesel programme for both the transport and industrial sectors. The association said that Indonesia and Thailand have announced measures to further advance their national biodiesel programmes by increasing biodiesel blending levels. MBA believed that, by leveraging Malaysia’s status as a leading palm oil producer, the government should further encourage the use of biodiesel outside the national biodiesel blending programme by exempting the 10 per cent sales tax on biodiesel.

items.title: Ipoh man, 25, dies in Buntong machete brawl; cops nab one suspect, looking for two more
items.summary: IPOH, April 7 — A 25-year-old man was found dead with slash wounds while some of his friends were injured in a machete attack in the Buntong area here yesterday. Ipoh police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said they received information about an argument at about 11.10 pm and dispatched a team to the scene. “Upon inspection, we found a man lying on the road with severe injuries from a sharp weapon.

items.title: Widow claiming royal connections lands in dock over obscene posts on TikTok, Instagram targeting Sultan Ibrahim and Brunei Ruler
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 7 — A 49-year-old single mother who claimed to be a teacher to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim was charged with sending obscene messages insulting Malaysian and Brunei royalty today. Nurul Huda Mohd Bajuri pled not guilty after the two charges were read out before Sessions Court Judge Mohd Azhar Othman. On the first charge, Nurul Huda was accused of knowingly transmitting obscene messages via TikTok under the account Ratu Nur Putri Ratu Balqis, where she claimed to be a teacher to Sultan Ibrahim, the Johor ruler, as well as other state monarchs, with the intention of upsetting others.

items.title: Kelantan abuse-of-power claims? ROS says 2017–2018 complaints investigated and closed in same year
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 7 — Investigations into complaints of misconduct and abuse of power involving several organisations in Kelantan received in 2017 and 2018 were resolved in the same year in accordance with the provisions under the Societies Act 1966, according to the Registrar of Societies Malaysia (ROS). In a statement today, the ROS said the investigation included the examination of related documents and representations from several involved parties, with appropriate enforcement actions taken against the relevant organisations in accordance with existing legal provisions, in addition to official notifications being conveyed to the complainants regarding the investigation results. “The ROS received written complaints regarding financial management and organisational governance issues in 2017 and 2018.

items.title: Like, share, earn? Bukit Aman warns Malaysians after RM251m lost to online part-time job scam since Jan 2025
items.summary: He said that a total of 10,304 cases were recorded during that period, involving losses of approximately RM251.2 million, with Selangor recording the highest number of cases and losses, namely 2,684 cases involving losses of RM80.18 million, followed by Kuala Lumpur with 1,170 cases (RM36.47 million) and Johor recording 1,443 cases (RM27.31 million). “For the period from Jan 1 to March 15, the age group of 31 to 40 years recorded the highest number of victims, with 435 cases, followed by the 21 to 30 years group (388), 41 to 50 years (289), 15 to 20 years (108), 51 to 60 years (100), and 61 years and above (56).” Commenting further, he said that women comprise most of the victims, with 6,734 people in 2025 compared to 2,194 men, whereas in the early period of 2026, 930 women were recorded compared to 446 men. “Civil servants also recorded 717 victims, followed by retirees (320 people), business people (370), teachers (245), private clerks (244), government clerks (62), and the security sector (eight victims).

items.title: Malaysia looks to Singapore, Australia, Zimbabwe for new harmony and reconciliation Bill, eyes mediation over punishment
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 7 — The drafting of the National Harmony and Reconciliation Commission (SKRN) bill, led by the Department of National Unity and Integration (JPNIN), will focus on preventive and conciliatory measures. The Ministry of National Unity (KPN) said the move comes in response to recent developments and growing controversies over unity issues that have threatened social harmony. “The ministry will ensure the commission can investigate issues affecting social harmony, summon relevant parties to give evidence, and issue recommendations and preventive measures to the government and relevant agencies,” the statement added.

items.title: Penang quit rent for 12ha plot slashed from RM334,544 to RM180, owners urged to appeal by April 30
items.summary: Major cuts confirmed: Penang has reduced quit rent rates by 78.5 to 82 per cent, including a case where a 12-hectare plot dropped from RM334,544 to RM180 after review. Appeals key to savings: Landowners must file appeals by April 30 to benefit from the reductions; the RM50 appeal fee has been waived. More changes in pipeline: The state is considering further measures, including a 50 per cent rebate this year and revised rates for low-cost housing, villages, and non-profit land use.

items.title: ‘Not guilty’ plea from ‘boss.s_k’ Instagramer charged with posting offensive content online
items.summary: SEPANG, April 7 — A trader pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to charges of creating and posting offensive content via social media two years ago. Sanuzi Kernain, 42, the accused, entered the plea after the charge was read before Judge Ahmad Fuad Othman. MCMC prosecutor Nazrul Nizam Mohd Zameri proposed bail of RM10,000 with one surety.

items.title: Anwar to urge Asean, China to fast-track South China Sea Code of Conduct at May summit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said he will urge his regional counterparts and China to expedite the conclusion of Code of Conduct (COC) on the South China Sea at the Asean Leaders’ Summit in Philippines next month. During the 2025 Asean Summit in Kuala Lumpur last October, Anwar said Chinese Premier Li Qiang has agreed to expedite talks on the COC. Asean and China signed the non-binding Declaration on the Conduct of Asean and China (DOC) in 2002 and initiated talks for a legally-binding COC in 2013.

items.title: Penang plans new state administrative centre, legislative assembly on Silicon Island
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 7 — The Penang government is planning the construction of a new state administrative centre including a new state legislative assembly on Silicon Island. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the Penang Development Corporation (PDC) was appointed to assess spatial and design requirements from various state agencies. He said data collection is underway to enable detailed architectural and land use planning for the administrative centre.

items.title: Former Johor property executive pleads not guilty to RM1m CBT charges
items.summary: Rafidah Abdul Hamid, 48, pleaded not guilty after the charges were read out before Judge Datuk Che Wan Zaidi Che Wan Ibrahim. According to the charge sheet, the accused is charged with committing criminal breach of trust by fraudulently using money, amounting to between RM73,000 and RM317,610, for her own benefit. while the accused unrepresented.
